generated_oml:
instance IncubatorTempSensorInside1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 1‑Wire digital thermometer located inside the incubator (±0.5 °C accuracy)",
    DTDFVocab:producedData IncubatorTempDataMessage1
]

instance IncubatorTempSensorInside2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 1‑Wire digital thermometer located inside the incubator (second sensor, ±0.5 °C accuracy)",
    DTDFVocab:producedData IncubatorTempDataMessage2
]

instance AmbientTempSensorRoom : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 1‑Wire digital thermometer measuring ambient room temperature (±0.5 °C accuracy)",
    DTDFVocab:producedData AmbientTempDataMessage
]

instance IncubatorTempDataMessage1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IncubatorTempSensorInside1
]

instance IncubatorTempDataMessage2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IncubatorTempSensorInside2
]

instance AmbientTempDataMessage : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AmbientTempSensorRoom
]

instance RabbitMQBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ AMQP broker used by the low‑level Python driver to publish temperature readings and status events",
    DTDFVocab:enables TemperatureSimulationService,
    ModelCalibrationService,
    RealTimeMonitoringService,
    SafetyViolationDetectionService
]

instance PythonLowLevelDriver : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python driver on Raspberry Pi that reads DS18S20 sensors and publishes messages to RabbitMQ",
    DTDFVocab:enables TemperatureSimulationService
]

instance ControllerBangBang : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Bang‑bang control logic (with waiting) implemented in Python, consumes sensor data from RabbitMQ to drive heat‑bed and fan",
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService,
    SafetyViolationDetectionService
]

instance SciPyOptimiser : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SciPy non‑linear least‑squares optimiser used for calibrating Model A and Model B parameters",
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelCalibrationService
]

instance IncubatorTempData1 : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Temperature time‑series (°C) from inside‑incubator sensor 1",
    DTDFVocab:fromData IncubatorTempDataMessage1,
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQBroker
]

instance IncubatorTempData2 : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Temperature time‑series (°C) from inside‑incubator sensor 2",
    DTDFVocab:fromData IncubatorTempDataMessage2,
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQBroker
]

instance AmbientTempData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Temperature time‑series (°C) from ambient room sensor",
    DTDFVocab:fromData AmbientTempDataMessage,
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQBroker
]

instance HeatBedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "MOSFET driver on custom PCB that switches the surface heater (≤4 A) via Raspberry Pi GPIO"
]

instance InternalFanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "MOSFET driver on custom PCB that switches the internal circulation fan via Raspberry Pi GPIO"
]

instance TemperatureSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Model‑based simulation of box temperature using calibrated thermal models",
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperaturePredictionInsight
]

instance ModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Calibration of Model A and Model B parameters against experimental temperature trajectories",
    DTDFVocab:provides CalibrationParameterInsight
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Realtime monitoring of temperature limits and generation of safety alerts",
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyAlertInsight
]

instance SafetyViolationDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Detection of temperature violations and triggering of human or automated interventions",
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyAlertInsight,
    AutomatedControlAdjustmentAction
]

instance TemperaturePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted temperature evolution of the incubator box based on calibrated thermal models"
]

instance CalibrationParameterInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Updated model parameters (C_air, G_box, C_heater, G_heater) after calibration"
]

instance SafetyAlertInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Alert indicating temperature has exceeded defined upper or lower limits"
]

instance HumanInterventionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Prompt operator to manually adjust heater or fan settings",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance AutomatedControlAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatically modify PWM signals to heater or fan to restore temperature within limits",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ThermalModelA : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model A: single thermal capacitance representing air volume with box heat‑loss parameter G_box",
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SciPyOptimiser
]

instance ThermalModelB : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model B: adds heater thermal capacitance C_heater and heat‑transfer parameter G_heater to Model A",
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SciPyOptimiser
]

instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains StyrofoamBox,
    HeatBedHeater,
    InternalFan,
    RaspberryPiController,
    CustomPCB,
    PowerSupply
]

instance StyrofoamBox : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Styrofoam insulated box (~0.03 m³ volume) forming the incubator enclosure"
]

instance HeatBedHeater : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Surface heater embedded in the box floor, driven up to 4 A"
]

instance InternalFan : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Internal fan for air circulation inside the incubator"
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi 4 Model B providing GPIO control and running Python driver & controller"
]

instance CustomPCB : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Custom printed circuit board with MOSFET drivers and optocouplers for heater and fan"
]

instance PowerSupply : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Power supply unit delivering required voltage and current to the incubator electronics"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "DT components publish command messages (heat‑bed ON/OFF, fan speed setpoints) to the RabbitMQ broker; the controller on the Raspberry Pi consumes these messages and, via the low‑level driver, drives the MOSFETs to actuate the heat‑bed and fan."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The architecture comprises the PT (incubator hardware), a low‑level driver that abstracts sensor/actuator I/O, a controller running on the Raspberry Pi, a RabbitMQ communication server on the same Pi, and external DT services that subscribe to the broker to receive sensor data, run simulations, and publish control commands; all components interact via the message bus."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is governed by four assumptions (uniform temperature, negligible wall heat accumulation, constant air specific heat, negligible heat losses from fan/heat‑bed). Validation is performed through uniform‑temperature experiments (comparing three sensor readings at 12 V and 18 V fan voltages) and by calibrating model parameters to minimise residual error between measured and simulated temperature trajectories."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Communication between PT and DT components uses the RabbitMQ message broker (AMQP protocol) running on the Raspberry Pi; messages are exchanged over the local network (Wi‑Fi or Ethernet) to which the Pi is connected."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The RabbitMQ server and the controller software are hosted on the same Raspberry Pi 4 Model B that also runs the low‑level driver; the PCB and sensors are physically attached to this node, providing an embedded deployment of the DT."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The low‑level driver incorporates a safe protocol to keep the device in a safe state; the controller enforces lower and upper temperature limits (LL, UL) and includes waiting durations to prevent overshoot, thereby providing runtime safety monitoring."
]
